engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 3008 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 3008 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The DV5RC BlueHDi replaced the DV6 in Gen 2, adding AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance but introducing new injector and turbo seal failure points.
commonFailures: AdBlue injector clogging causing limp mode (80k+ miles)
Turbo oil feed line coking leading to bearing wear
EGR valve carbon buildup affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5RC Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The workhorse 1.6L HDi of early 3008s, widely used but prone to turbo and EGR issues on high-mileage examples.
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leak causing blue smoke and oil loss
EGR cooler failure leading to coolant loss
Injector seal wear causing diesel knock
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6CED4 Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The performance diesel for GT and high-spec trims, offering strong torque but requiring careful DPF maintenance to avoid regeneration failures.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure causing power loss
EGR cooler leak leading to coolant contamination
Injector seal wear causing diesel dilution of oil
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10FC Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The newer, more efficient 1.5L BlueHDi introduced in 2019, offering improved emissions but with limited long-term reliability data.
commonFailures: Injector seal wear causing oil dilution
Timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileage
AdBlue system sensor faults triggering warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for EH5 Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The updated PureTech engine with revised timing components, though chain tensioner wear remains a documented concern requiring vigilant maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Access, Active, Allure, GT Line, GT
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling on cold start (60k-100k miles)
Piston ring wear leading to oil consumption
Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP5 Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The turbocharged THP variant delivered strong performance but required meticulous maintenance to avoid timing chain and fuel system failures.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing valve timing loss

entries.title: pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure leading to no-start
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated VTi offered simplicity and lower running costs but suffered from carbon buildup and VANOS solenoid issues.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves causing rough idle
VANOS solenoid failure affecting variable valve timing
Water pump seal leak leading to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6 Peugeot 3008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Hybrid
entries.history: The Hybrid4 system paired a diesel engine with a rear electric motor for all-wheel drive capability, though complexity increases replacement considerations.

entries.title: voltage battery degradation reducing electric range
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10BTED4 Peugeot 3008 Hybrid engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Plug-in Hybrid
entries.history: The latest Plug-in Hybrid combines a turbo petrol engine with a more powerful electric motor for extended electric-only driving and lower emissions.

entries.title: in Hybrid Allure, GT Line, GT
voltage battery capacity loss affecting electric range

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 3008 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 3008's most common engine issues centre on the 1.2 PureTech timing chain, 1.6/2.0 BlueHDi turbo and EGR systems, and early-generation THP petrol units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section as your decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Peugeot 3008 PureTech Timing Chain Failure - Why 1.2L Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 3008 1.2 PureTech Active, Allure, GT Line (2016-2024, EP3/EP5 codes HMP, HNS)
problems.rootCause: The EP3/EP5 timing chain tensioner can wear prematurely, allowing chain slack that leads to skipped teeth, valve timing loss, and catastrophic piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early via noise diagnosis; not suitable post-failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once interference damage occurs; most cost-effective long-term solution

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start from your 1.2 PureTech, act immediately - a preventative chain kit replacement can save the engine. Once metallic knocking or loss of power occurs, replacement is the only viable option. On a 3008 worth £8,000-£15,000, a rebuilt PureTech engine offers better value than dealer repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP5 PureTech engine prices for your Peugeot 3008

problems.h4: Peugeot 3008 BlueHDi Turbo Failure - Why DV5/DW10 Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 3008 1.6/2.0 BlueHDi Active, Allure, GT (2009-2024, DV5RC, DV6CED4, DW10FC codes)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil feed lines can coke up from short-journey driving, starving the turbo of lubrication and causing bearing wear, shaft play, and eventual boost loss or catastrophic seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger, clean oil feed, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine internals undamaged; risk of repeat failure if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, oil system flush, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo debris entered sump or engine shows low compression; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: BlueHDi turbos fail most often on vehicles used for short trips. If your 3008 shows blue smoke, loss of power, or whining under boost, get a compression test first. If compression is good, a specialist turbo replacement may suffice. If not, a rebuilt BlueHDi engine with upgraded oil feed components is the smarter investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 3008 BlueHDi replacement engines

problems.h4: Peugeot 3008 EGR/DPF Engine Damage - Why Diesel Engines Seize
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 3008 1.6/2.0 HDi/BlueHDi all trims (2009-2024, DV6, DW10, EH5 codes)
problems.rootCause: Clogged EGR valves and failed DPF regenerations cause excessive exhaust backpressure and oil dilution, leading to bearing wear, sludge buildup, and eventual engine seizure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean/replace EGR valve, force DPF regeneration, full synthetic oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if engine still runs smoothly with no knock; preventative maintenance, not repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated EGR/DPF components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential once knocking, low oil pressure, or seizure occurs; only reliable long-term fix

problems.recommendation: EGR and DPF issues are preventable with regular motorway driving and timely servicing. If your 3008 diesel shows warning lights, get diagnostics immediately. Once the engine knocks or seizes, no amount of EGR cleaning will help - a rebuilt BlueHDi engine with updated emission components is your only path back on the road.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 3008 diesel engine replacement prices

problems.h4: Peugeot 3008 THP Petrol Engine Failure - Why EP6 Units Fail
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 3008 1.6 THP Active, Allure, GT Line (2009-2016, EP6DT/5FN codes)
problems.rootCause: The EP6DT timing chain tensioner and high-pressure fuel pump are prone to premature wear, causing chain jump, valve damage, or fuel system contamination that leads to catastrophic failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides, HPFP filter
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if diagnosed early via rattling noise; not suitable post-failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded chain components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once valve timing is lost or compression is low; most reliable solution

problems.recommendation: Early EP6 THP engines require vigilant maintenance. If your 3008 1.6 THP rattles on cold start or shows misfires, act fast. A preventative chain kit can save the engine. Once damage occurs, a rebuilt THP engine with upgraded components offers better value and reliability than repeated dealer repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6DT engine prices for your Peugeot 3008

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 3008
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with PureTech timing chain wear, BlueHDi turbo failure, or THP petrol issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 3008 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components that address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 3008 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 3008 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi (EH5) arrived in later Gen 2 models as a refined, efficient diesel option offering improved emissions and fuel economy over the older 1.6L units, primarily fitted to Active and Allure trims.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 3008 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 3008 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi/BlueHDi (DV6/DV5) is the most common diesel engine in the 3008 range, powering entry-level to mid-spec trims across both generations with proven economy but known turbo and EGR vulnerabilities.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 3008 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 3008 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi/BlueHDi (DW10) serves as the performance diesel option for Allure, GT Line and GT trims, delivering strong torque for towing and motorway driving with robust construction but DPF-related failure risks.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 3008 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 3008 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech (EP3/EP5) is the dominant petrol engine in Gen 2 3008s, offering excellent fuel economy but requiring vigilant maintenance due to the well-documented timing chain wear issue affecting EP-family engines.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 3008 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 3008 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L VTi/THP (EP6) powered early Gen 1 petrol 3008s, with naturally aspirated VTi offering simplicity and turbocharged THP delivering performance, though both require careful maintenance to avoid timing and fuel system issues.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 3008 1.6L petrol engine prices

EH5 - newer 1.5L BlueHDi (2019+), improved emissions but injector seal wear
foundIn: Gen 2 (2016-2024) - DV5RC 1.6L BlueHDi, DW10FC 2.0L BlueHDi, EH5 1.5L BlueHDi
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy and towing capability
Turbo oil seal leaks and EGR clogging on high-mileage examples
AdBlue system faults requiring specialist diagnostics on BlueHDi units
High UK replacement demand on 1.6L variants due to parc volume

items.title: Peugeot 3008 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol 3008s use 1.2L PureTech or 1.6L THP/VTi engines, offering smoother refinement but requiring vigilant timing chain and oil maintenance to avoid costly failures.
items.descriptor: Petrol 3008s use 1.2L PureTech or 1.6L THP/VTi engines, offering smoother refinement but requiring vigilant timing chain and oil maintenance to avoid costly failures.
families: EP5/HNS - updated 1.2L PureTech (2019+), improved but still timing-chain sensitive
EP6DT/5FN - turbocharged 1.6L THP, performance-oriented but HPFP and chain risks
EP6/5FS - naturally aspirated 1.6L VTi, simpler but carbon buildup prone
knownFor: Smooth, quiet operation and lower road tax vs diesel
PureTech timing chain tensioner wear between 60k-100k miles
THP high-pressure fuel pump and turbo wastegate failures
Oil consumption issues on early PureTech units requiring monitoring

items.title: Peugeot 3008 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: Hybrid4 and Plug-in Hybrid variants combine combustion engines with electric motors for improved efficiency, though replacement requires specialist hybrid system knowledge.
items.descriptor: Hybrid4 and Plug-in Hybrid variants combine combustion engines with electric motors for improved efficiency, though replacement requires specialist hybrid system knowledge.
items.familiesLabel: H2: Peugeot 3008 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
families: DW10BTED4+Electric - Hybrid4 diesel-electric (2012-2016), complex but capable
EP6FDT+Electric - Plug-in Hybrid petrol-electric (2020-2024), latest technology
Hybrid4: Rear electric motor provides all-wheel drive and electric-only low-speed capability
PHEV: Front-mounted electric motor integrated with petrol engine for seamless power delivery
Both systems recover energy via regenerative braking to recharge the high-voltage battery
foundIn: Plug-in Hybrid Allure/GT/GT Line (2020-2024) - current generation
importantNotes: Hybrid battery health check essential during engine replacement
Specialist diagnostics required for high-voltage system safety
Availability more limited than standard engines; allow extra lead time

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 3008 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 3008's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Gen 1 Launch: HDi Diesel & THP Petrol Debut
years.description: The original 3008 launched with proven HDi diesel and THP petrol engines, establishing the model's reputation for practicality and refinement.
keyChanges: Peugeot 3008 first launched in UK with crossover SUV styling
Initial engine lineup: 1.6L HDi diesel, 2.0L HDi diesel, 1.6L VTi/THP petrol
HYbrid4 concept announced but not yet in production
knownFor: Spacious interior and versatile cabin layout
Early HDi units robust but EGR/turbo issues emerge at high mileage
THP engines require careful maintenance to avoid timing chain problems
Strong used market demand for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Peugeot 3008 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 3008 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 3008 THP timing chain • Peugeot 3008 2.0 HDi turbo failure

years.preview: Gen 1 Facelift: BlueHDi Introduction & Hybrid4 Arrival
years.description: The facelifted 3008 introduced BlueHDi technology for lower emissions and the innovative Hybrid4 diesel-electric powertrain for enhanced capability.
keyChanges: Facelift styling with revised front end and interior updates
BlueHDi engines introduced with AdBlue for Euro 6 compliance
HYbrid4 production model launched with rear electric motor
THP engines received minor revisions to improve reliability
knownFor: BlueHDi offers improved fuel economy but adds AdBlue system complexity
Hybrid4 provides unique all-wheel drive capability with electric boost
THP engines still require vigilant timing chain maintenance
Strong demand for facelift models with updated tech and efficiency
years.ticker: Peugeot 3008 BlueHDi AdBlue fault • Peugeot 3008 Hybrid4 battery replacement • Peugeot 3008 THP HPFP failure

years.preview: Gen 2 Launch: PureTech & New BlueHDi Families
years.description: The all-new second-generation 3008 brought a modern platform, updated PureTech petrol engines and revised BlueHDi diesels with improved efficiency.
keyChanges: All-new Gen 2 platform with i-Cockpit interior and SUV styling
New EP3/EP5 PureTech 1.2L petrol engines replace older 1.6L units
Revised DV5RC 1.6L BlueHDi and DW10FC 2.0L BlueHDi diesels
Enhanced infotainment and driver assistance systems
knownFor: Modern interior and improved driving dynamics vs Gen 1
PureTech engines offer excellent economy but timing chain wear concerns emerge
BlueHDi units more refined but require AdBlue top-ups and DPF maintenance
Strong resale values and high UK registration numbers
years.cta: Get quotes for Gen 2 Peugeot 3008 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 3008 PureTech timing chain • Peugeot 3008 1.6 BlueHDi turbo • Peugeot 3008 2.0 BlueHDi DPF

years.description: The updated Gen 2 introduced Plug-in Hybrid variants and the new 1.5L BlueHDi engine, expanding efficiency options while maintaining performance.
keyChanges: Plug-in Hybrid variants launched with extended electric-only range
New EH5 1.5L BlueHDi engine introduced for improved emissions
Revised infotainment with larger screens and connected services
Minor styling updates and enhanced driver assistance features
mainEngines: EH5/Y01 - 1.5L BlueHDi diesel (new for 2019+, Active/Allure/GT Line)
DV5RC - 1.6L BlueHDi diesel (continued on some trims)
knownFor: Plug-in Hybrid offers compelling company car tax benefits and low running costs
1.5L BlueHDi provides improved efficiency but limited long-term reliability data
PureTech timing chain concerns persist; early diagnosis critical
Latest models command strong residuals but replacement parts can be costlier

faq.h2: Peugeot 3008 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 3008 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Peugeot 3008 engine typically costs £1,200-£3,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour. PureTech and BlueHDi units dominate pricing searches.
keyPoints: 1.2 PureTech rebuilt: £1,500-£3,200 supply-only
1.6 BlueHDi rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800 supply-only
2.0 BlueHDi rebuilt: £1,900-£3,500 supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£900
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 3008 engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 3008?
items.answer: Common Peugeot 3008 engine codes include DV5RC (1.6 BlueHDi), EP5 (1.2 PureTech), DW10FC (2.0 BlueHDi), and EH5 (1.5 BlueHDi). Verify via VIN or engine bay sticker.
keyPoints: Diesel codes: DV5RC, DV6CED4, DW10FC, EH5
Petrol codes: EP3, EP5, EP6, EP6DT
Hybrid codes: DW10BTED4+Electric, EP6FDT+Electric
Always cross-reference with VIN for exact fitment
items.cta: Check Peugeot 3008 engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 3008 engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your 3008 is otherwise in good condition. A rebuilt engine costing £1,500-£3,000 is often better value than scrapping a £8,000-£15,000 SUV, especially with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
Replacement extends vehicle life by 80,000-120,000 miles typically
Compare replacement cost vs vehicle market value before deciding
Supply & fit options available nationwide for convenience
items.cta: Find out if Peugeot 3008 engine replacement is right for you

items.question: Do Peugeot 3008 PureTech engines have timing chain problems?
items.answer: Yes, EP3/EP5 PureTech engines can suffer timing chain tensioner wear between 60,000-100,000 miles. Listen for rattling on cold start and act early to avoid catastrophic damage.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner is the common failure point, not the chain itself
Rattling noise on cold start is the key warning symptom
Preventative replacement costs £500-£900; post-failure rebuild £1,500+
Rebuilt PureTech engines include upgraded tensioner components
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 3008 PureTech timing chain replacement

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 3008 engine?
items.answer: The 2.0L BlueHDi (DW10FC) is generally considered the most robust Peugeot 3008 engine when properly maintained, offering strong torque and fewer high-mileage issues than smaller units.
keyPoints: 2.0 BlueHDi benefits from over-engineered construction
Regular oil changes and DPF maintenance critical for longevity
1.6 BlueHDi also reliable but more sensitive to short-journey use
Avoid early PureTech units without timing chain service history
items.cta: Compare reliable Peugeot 3008 engine replacement options

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 3008 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 2-5 working days. Supply & fit installation requires 1-3 days at a specialist garage, depending on variant complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Standard diesel/petrol engines: 2-3 days fitting time
Hybrid/PHEV units: 3-5 days due to high-voltage system procedures
AdBlue system calibration required on BlueHDi replacements
Book specialist fitting in advance to minimise downtime
items.cta: Book Peugeot 3008 engine supply & fit appointment

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 3008 replacement engine?
items.answer: Engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Terms vary by supplier, so confirm coverage details before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Unlimited mileage coverage on most specialist-supplied engines
Labour warranty may be separate; confirm with fitting garage
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: View Peugeot 3008 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot 3008?
items.answer: Engine swaps are technically possible but not recommended for the 3008 due to complex ECU integration, emissions compliance, and insurance implications. Stick to code-matched replacements.
keyPoints: ECU immobiliser and AdBlue systems require code-matched engines
Non-standard swaps may fail MOT emissions testing
Insurance may be voided by unapproved engine modifications
Use our code checker to find exact compatible replacements
items.cta: Verify Peugeot 3008 engine compatibility before ordering

items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Peugeot 3008 engine specialists offering rebuilt, reconditioned and used options with price comparison, warranty coverage and supply & fit services.
keyPoints: Compare quotes from 100+ UK specialists in one place
All suppliers vetted for quality, warranty and customer service
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failures
Nationwide delivery and fitting network available
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 3008 engine suppliers UK now

items.question: How to find my Peugeot 3008 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 3008 engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the oil filter housing) and listed on the VIN plate in the driver's door jamb or service book.
keyPoints: Check engine bay sticker near oil filter or timing cover
VIN plate in driver's door jamb lists engine code
Service book or MOT certificate may record engine details
Enter registration on EngineMarket for instant code verification
